mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of rate-increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market triggers recession expectations, causing investors to de-risk equity portfolios and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where unemployment rises > 0.2pp over a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate signals robust consumer spending power without monetary headwinds, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in > 55% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher near-term earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns during recovery phases or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market and rising aggregate income, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative equity returns in > 50% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds the 305.0 threshold, it signals entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 305.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production confirms broad-based economic momentum, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative returns in > 55% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate profit margins, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP acceleration fails to predict positive returns in early-cycle regimes or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.
